concept_name: Target-Aware Governed Prompt Compilation subsystem evaluation (GH #39)

spark.observation: Horizontal multi-provider prompt optimization is wasteful when target is known. Filer proposes Qor-logic compile vertically: intent -> canonical IR -> governance -> target resolution -> provider compilation.
spark.initial_question: Should Qor-logic expand from governance skills to deterministic target-aware prompt compiler as a Python package subsystem?
spark.why_now: Multi-agent federation use cases emerging in 2026; differentiates Qor-logic from prompt prettifiers.

affected_actors: Operators using Qor-logic as governance shim around prompt execution
problem_frame.failure_mode: Two blended framings: wasteful prompt optimization + Qor-logic strategic identity. Failing to address (1) means consumers re-implement; failing to scope (2) carefully dilutes existing skill focus.
problem_frame.cost_of_failure: Either consumers continue re-implementing or 5-phase implementation dilutes maintainer attention from existing SDLC skills.

transformation_statement: Qor-logic moves from governance-framework to (A) same + target-aware prompt compiler OR (B) same with explicit out-of-scope declaration -- without diluting skill/doctrine surface or claiming product identity implementation can't support.

non_goals: NOT shipping rulepacks for every provider in V1
limitations: V1 exploratory: minimum viable IR + 1 provider
exclusions: No changes to existing SDLC skills
forbidden_interpretations: NOT permission to rewrite Qor-logic as primarily compiler
Target-aware does NOT mean model router
NOT a hard commitment to Phases 2-5

options_matrix.name: Accept (vertical compiler subsystem)
options_matrix.rejection_reason: Largest scope expansion; real risk of creep into all 5 phases without validation.

options_matrix.name: Accept as exploratory probe
options_matrix.summary: Minimal Spike: PromptIR + ParsedIntent schemas + stub compile_prompt().
options_matrix.selection_reason: Smallest reversible step; validates IR shape; forward-compatible.

options_matrix.name: Defer (out-of-scope)
options_matrix.summary: Doctrine entry: prompt-compilation out of scope.

options_matrix.name: Pilot in consumer
options_matrix.summary: Build in sibling project; contribute upstream if validated.
options_matrix.rejection_reason: Lowest commitment but loses first-mover position; fallback if Option 2 spike returns negative.

evidence_required: Spike PromptIR + ParsedIntent schemas + stub compile_prompt
escalation_triggers: If PromptIR requires provider-specific shape, escalate back to /qor-ideate (proposal's premise broken; Option 3 becomes right answer)
If spike shows zero demand, escalate to /qor-remediate to close with disposition in doctrine
If demand strong but API needs rework, escalate to /qor-refactor before Phase 1

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Spike API shape doesn't fit consumer use case
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Consumer integration report flags critical gaps
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Re-author IR schema with consumer-driven shape; re-run spike

failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: compiler vs skill reference count threshold breach in README/CONCEPT/CHANGELOG
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Re-anchor with doctrine asserting governance-primary identity

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Provider rulepack maintenance burden exceeds capacity
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Pause new provider onboarding; community contributions; or dynamic doc lookup

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Governance gate cannot be deterministic for prompt-injection
failure_remediation_plan.detection_signal: Gate test fails on adversarial inputs humans classify correctly
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Escalate to /qor-audit for prompt-injection pass extension; hybrid LLM-assisted triage policy carve-out

failure_remediation_plan.failure_class: Compare-mode usage drifts from explicit benchmarking to default fallback
failure_remediation_plan.containment_action: Add friction (operator confirmation); doctrine entry codifies anti-pattern

readiness.rationale: Unlike #38/#51 (concrete failures), #39 is strategic architecture with 4 open questions + meta-question of scope-fit. /qor-research must survey existing projects (LangChain, DSPy, Guidance), validate API shape with consumers, examine reuse of existing governance hooks, return spike-OK or defer-recommended.
